parsed.answer: No, 'cage-free' does not guarantee outdoor access. Cage-free hens are housed without battery cages and have freedom to move within an indoor facility, but they are typically confined indoors. Outdoor access is a feature of other labels like 'free-range' or 'pasture-raised,' which have different (and generally higher) welfare standards.
